flowTitle: XIIGen Platform — Profile Enrichment & Matching Intelligence

freedomConfigAdaptation.description: Per-tenant configurable values. Super-tenant sets defaults at fork; sub-tenant overrides at install. MACHINE logic around them does not change.

keys.semantic: Debounce window for QuestionnaireCompleted deduplication. Tenants with chatty upstream clients can raise this; tenants with strict UX latency can lower it.
Timeout for compatibility scoring (B1) — after this, partialResults=true is returned. Timeout-as-success pattern; MACHINE behavior around timeout is fixed.

boundedRange.rationale: 5s floor for realistic AI call; 2min ceiling for user-perceived latency budget

keys.semantic: Contribution of `industry_code` match to the compatibility score. Tenants with industry-specific communities (e.g. legal-tech-only) may weight this higher.
Contribution of `business_stage` match (early / growth / mature). Tenants whose communities span multiple stages may lower this.
Contribution of `location_proximity` match. Remote-first tenants lower; region-focused tenants raise.
Contribution of `team_size_tier` match. Tenants of mostly solopreneurs lower; enterprise tenants raise.

freedomConfigAdaptation.consistencyRule: The four match weights (industry + stage + location + team) MUST sum to 1.0. Enforced by contract invariant — NOT MACHINE logic; just a registry consistency gate.

grammarAdaptation.notApplicableReason: FLOW-02 has no DSL grammar surface. Matches FLOW-01 stance — profile-enrichment is data pipeline + scoring, not form-condition evaluation.

roleScopeAdaptation.description: Role-visibility matrices for client-side access control. Uses ViewerRole via @xiigen/plugin-sdk workspace package.

keys.semantic: Member can complete their own questionnaire; admin sees + can edit any member's answers for moderation
keys.extensionPoint: ViewerRole-aware render; tenants can override per-role visibility of specific questionnaire fields via FREEDOM config (future — not in this session)

keys.semantic: Compatibility scores are private except to the two members involved + admin
keys.extensionPoint: Confidence-display threshold per role — tenants can set ADMIN-only visibility of raw numerical score; MEMBER sees only qualitative label

keys.semantic: Personalization reasoning is shown only to the member; admin audit view shows without reasoning
keys.extensionPoint: Show/hide reasoning toggle per role — FREEDOM-configurable

dependencies: ViewerRole primitive from plugin-sdk — already extracted at client/src/components/common/ViewerRole.ts

businessDomainAdaptation.description: Business-domain edge cases a tenant can override through parameters + FREEDOM.

edgeCases.name: Timeout returns partialResults=true (not failure)
edgeCases.semantic: B1 scoring caller passes `timeoutMs` (default from `flow02_match_timeout_seconds`). On timeout, `partialResults=true` flows downstream. Tenants cannot change this pattern (MACHINE) but can tune the timeout window.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Timeout window tunable; timeout-as-success behavior fixed.

edgeCases.name: Missing industry → segment=GENERAL
edgeCases.semantic: Analytics segmentation returns `segment='GENERAL'` with `degraded=true` when industry missing. Tenants cannot disable GENERAL fallback (MACHINE) but can map their own taxonomies to the input industry field.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: Upstream questionnaire mapping (out of flow scope)
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Always succeeds; GENERAL fallback is MACHINE.

edgeCases.name: Per-channel consent gate on T52 community broadcast
edgeCases.semantic: Each community channel (Slack/community-feed/etc) requires explicit consent per member. Tenants cannot bypass consent (MACHINE + IR-1 + CF-801) but can define the enabled channel list via FREEDOM.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: flow02_broadcast_channels_enabled FREEDOM key (registered in contract, pending registry alignment per ISSUE-002)
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Consent gate per channel is MACHINE; channel list tunable.

ironRulesMACHINE.description: Fixed architectural invariants. Tenants cannot change these without breaking cascade safety, DNA compliance, or cross-flow contracts.
rules: Rule 1 / D-HIST-001: No direct SDK imports — fabric interfaces only (QG-02-01)
DNA-3: All methods return DataProcessResult<T> — never throw (QG-02-02)
DNA-5: Tenant scope via AsyncLocalStorage — never tenantId parameter (QG-02-03)
DNA-8: storeDocument() BEFORE enqueue() on every state transition (QG-02-04, QG-02-11, QG-02-22, QG-02-32)
DNA-7: Idempotency key format = sha256(tenantId + userId + 'business-profile')
T50 archetype is fan_in: Promise.allSettled, NEVER Promise.all (QG-02-10)
T51 confidence threshold from FREEDOM config — score-0 if hardcoded (QG-02-20, QG-02-23 + named check convergence_threshold_from_freedom_config)
T51 degraded terminal emits MatchingDeferred — NEVER DataProcessResult.failure (QG-02-21)
T52 consent checked per channel BEFORE broadcast emit (QG-02-30, CF-801)
T52 channel failure returns DataProcessResult.success — best-effort observer (QG-02-31, CF-799)
No PII in queue event payloads — userId + matched IDs only (QG-02-33, IR-1)
DUAL-RECORD-WRITE: A1 writes PRIVATE xiigen-business-profiles + GLOBAL xiigen-matching-profiles (4 match-safe fields only)
Cross-flow contract (FLOW-02-DR-02-C): FLOW-02 emits PersonalizationCompleted, NEVER OnboardingCompleted
Match weight registry consistency: flow02_match_weight_{industry,stage,location,team} MUST sum to 1.0

freedom-config-example-industry-tenant.description: A legal-tech-only tenant reweights matching toward industry + stage
freedom-config-example-industry-tenant.impact: Matches surface legal-industry-adjacent businesses first; MACHINE scoring logic unchanged.
freedom-config-example-industry-tenant.testable: Layer 1 phase-02-adaptation-freedom-config.spec.ts FC-ADAPT-1 asserts weights applied per-tenant.

freedom-config-example-aggressive-timeou.description: UX-strict tenant shortens scoring timeout from 30s to 8s
freedom-config-example-aggressive-timeou.impact: B1 more often returns partialResults=true; downstream B2/C/D handle partial gracefully (MACHINE).

notPartOfAdaptationSurface.description: Elements of FLOW-02 that are NOT tenant-adaptable.
elements: Event names (BusinessProfileCreated, BusinessMatchesFound, PersonalizationCompleted, MatchingConverged, MatchingDeferred, OnboardingBroadcastSent)
DUAL-RECORD-WRITE field set (profile_id, user_id, tenant_id, industry_code, business_stage, location_proximity, team_size_tier — fixed set)
Idempotency key format (sha256 hex, first 12 chars)

layer_2_playwright.conditional: Playwright harness availability — check before writing

layer_3_visual.delegation: UI/UX agent via Claude_Preview MCP. Screenshots saved under docs/portability/flow-02/visual-evidence/. Verdict returned to session chat. Images NEVER sent to chat.
cells: C1 default desktop 1440 LTR — Questionnaire + Matching + Personalization
C4 acme-pro-members desktop 1440 LTR (FREEDOM-override visual diff)
C6 role-scope: MEMBER vs ADMIN vs CONNECTION on MatchingPage
C7 degraded state: partialResults=true on MatchingPage + degraded=true on PersonalizationPage

tenantSeparation.detection: (a) grep process.env.* reads for tenant config; (b) grep secret patterns sk-ant- / sk-proj- / ghp_ / AIzaSy / pcsk_ in committed files; (c) tenant isolation spec proves acme-pro-members overrides do not leak to default tenant
tenantSeparation.expected: (a) 0 tenant-scoped env reads; (b) 0 secret-pattern matches in committed code; (c) isolation spec green

visualValidation.detection: UI/UX agent (SK-549 delegate) runs cells C1..C7; returns verdict table
visualValidation.expected: All cells PASS; 0 images in session chat; screenshots persisted under docs/portability/flow-02/visual-evidence/
